title: agoseris glauca subsp. aspera
intro: Agoseris glauca subsp. aspera, also known as the variegated desert agoseris or variegated mountain fleabane, is a rare and endemic plant species native to the southwestern United States. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Agoseris glauca subsp. aspera include its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its ability to alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and other respiratory conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its roots can be dried and made into a powder for medicinal use.
